@graph.name: BMW models fitted with the BMW N57

mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW N57 engine replacement cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned N57 typically costs £4,500–£7,000 supply only [BMW-QUOTE], varying by variant (single/twin/triple-turbo) and vehicle application. Fitting adds £780–£1,300 depending on labour rates. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the BMW N57 a reliable engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N57 earns 3.5★ out of 5 [BMW-VERIFIED], based on 830+ UK enquiries and cross-referenced specialist reports. It's more reliable than the N47 but less robust than the M57 it replaced.

mainEntity.name: What is the most common BMW N57 failure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch is the most serious — the chain can stretch, wear guides, and cause catastrophic engine failure if ignored. Typically appears from 80,000–150,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW models use the N57 engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: The N57 powers the 330d/335d (3 Series), 530d/535d/M550d (5 Series), 640d (6 Series), 730d/740d/750d (7 Series), and X3/X4/X5/X6 xDrive30d/35d/40d/M50d models.

mainEntity.name: How long does the BMW N57 engine last?

acceptedAnswer.text: With meticulous servicing — especially 8,000-mile oil changes using BMW Longlife-04 spec — an N57 can exceed 180,000–200,000 miles. Crankshaft bearing failures are rare outside police/high-idle duty cycles.

hero.h1: BMW N57 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The BMW N57 is BMW’s 3.0-litre straight-six turbodiesel — a strong performer, but timing chain wear and turbo failures are well documented. Backed by 830+ real UK enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

rows.value: 14 models (3, 4, 5, 6, 7 Series, X3, X4, X5, X6)

verdictRating.confidence: 90% confidence — based on 830 UK enquiries, specialist rebuild reports, and cross-referenced owner forums.

rows.score: 12 (Mix of cheap fixes (turbo, injectors) and expensive ones (timing chain, crankshaft bearings))
20 (Well-supported; parts available from multiple UK suppliers)
12 (Recon cost 45–59% of lowest host-vehicle value (sub-£10k F10/E70 examples))

verdictRating.oneLineVerdict: The N57 is a strong, refined diesel six — but timing chain stretch and turbo failures are genuine risks — buy only with full service history and a documented chain inspection.
verdictRating.bestFor: High-mileage motorway drivers who service on schedule with premium oil.
verdictRating.avoidIf: No documented timing chain history above 80,000 miles or signs of turbo wear.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Exclusive to BMW — no cross-brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an N57 typically takes 12–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE].
